name: ciana-parrot
description: Self-hosted AI assistant with multi-channel support, scheduled tasks, and extensible skills

commit.message:
refactor: cache repeated dict lookups and deduplicate tool branch

- Cache `data.get("message", {})` in local var in bridge NDJSON parsers
  to avoid redundant dictionary lookups
- Merge duplicate Bash/host_execute branches in summarize_tool_input()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add tests for conversation history preview

Cover get_conversation_messages bridge method and the updated
activate_conversation handler with history display.

feat: show conversation history when resuming a CC session

Display last 8 messages with user/assistant icons and total count
when activating an existing conversation from the inline keyboard.

feat: add get_conversation_messages() to Claude Code bridge

Read last N user/assistant messages from session JSONL files,
skipping tool-only assistant entries, for conversation preview.

refactor: remove redundant .keys() in set() call

`set(dict.keys())` → `set(dict)` — iterating a dict already yields keys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address PR #5 review — dead param, duplication, double-conversion

- Remove unused int_chat_id parameter from _cc_cmd_resume
- Deduplicate cc:cost by delegating to _cc_cmd_status with footer kwarg
- Fix double md_to_telegram_html conversion in _cc_cmd_memory

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge pull request #5 from MegliolaLorenzo/feat/cc-commands-improvements

Fix and extend Telegram cc: commands

Merge pull request #5 from MegliolaLorenzo/feat/cc-commands-improvements

Fix and extend Telegram cc: commands

commit.message:
feat: fix and extend Telegram cc: commands

- Fix HTML formatting: convert all cc: command output from raw HTML to
  Markdown syntax (was being double-escaped by md_to_telegram_html)
- Fix cc:model shortcut resolution: cc:model sonnet now correctly saves
  "claude-sonnet-4-6" instead of raw "sonnet"
- Improve cc:cost: show project, session, model, effort config instead
  of a static "not available" message
- Add cc:resume [id]: resume a session by partial ID or show conversation list
- Add cc:memory: show CLAUDE.md memory files via bridge
- Add cc:project [name]: quick project switch by partial name match
- Add cc:doctor: check gateway health status
- Add 61 tests covering all new and existing cc: commands

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
perf: avoid O(n) max() scan in update dedup cleanup
refactor: use set instead of list for allowed_users O(1) lookup
refactor: extract _ensure_utc helper in scheduler to DRY timezone handling

fix: remove redundant shell metacharacter rejection in backend

Safety relies on subprocess shell=False (list exec) + command allowlist.
Metacharacters in arguments are harmless since no shell interprets them.
Also updates AGENT.md to reflect host_execute/web_fetch tool names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: align CHANGELOG with Keep a Changelog format and add version links

- Reformat CHANGELOG to adhere to Keep a Changelog standards.
- Add version comparison links for easier navigation.

feat: introduce multi-tier model router with dynamic tier switching

- Add `RoutingChatModel` to handle tier-specific routing based on active context.
- Implement `switch_model` tool for dynamic in-conversation tier switching.
- Add `init_model_router_tools` for router initialization and tier model configuration.
- Expand `AGENT.md` with detailed guidance for `switch_model` usage.
- Update tests to validate model routing, tier context management, and tool behavior.
- Include support for local config overrides (`config.local.yaml`).

docs: add detailed skill creation guide and link to agent documentation

- Introduce comprehensive `SKILL.md` guide for creating, updating, and managing skills, including format specifications, examples, and rules.
- Update `AGENT.md` to reference the new skill creation guide, replacing redundant information.

docs: update README with docs site link, feature list refinements, and quick start adjustments

- Add documentation site badge and link (docs.cianaparrot.dev) to the README.
- Refine feature descriptions for clarity and conciseness.
- Streamline quick start section for improved user experience.

docs: add comprehensive user guides for bridges, channels, modes, and config

- Document how to add new host bridges, including setup, config, and security guidelines.
- Provide step-by-step guide for creating channel adapters like Discord.
- Add detailed steps for mode handler creation in Telegram, with practical use cases.
- Include instructions for extending the configuration system with new validated sections.
- Ensure consistency with existing documentation style.

fix(gateway): treat timeout=0 as no limit instead of default timeout

subprocess.run(timeout=None) disables the timeout entirely.
Previously timeout=0 fell back to DEFAULT_TIMEOUT (30s), causing
Claude Code requests to fail on complex or slow tasks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: improve tool display names, bridge icons, and session counter sync

- Show bridge name (e.g. "Whatsapp", "Spotify") instead of
  "host_execute" in Telegram tool-call rendering, with per-bridge
  emoji icons
- Add display_name field to ToolCallEvent and resolve_display_name()
  in events.py; extend summarize_tool_input for host_execute
- Fix session counter sync: restore to existing value on restart
  instead of bumping by one (prevented unnecessary session jumps)
- Add explicit checkpointer SQLite connection close on shutdown
- Fix Dockerfile healthcheck path to /app/data/checkpoints.db

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(whatsapp): add wacli-daemon for background sync management

Introduces wacli-daemon script (start/stop/status) to manage
wacli sync --follow as a persistent background process. Updates
SKILL.md with pre-flight checks so the agent verifies/starts the
daemon before any WhatsApp operation. Adds wacli-daemon to the
gateway bridge allowlist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: add pre-commit hook for gitleaks integration

- Configure `.pre-commit-config.yaml` with gitleaks to enforce secret scanning.

refactor: enhance error handling, add security measures, and improve validation

- Standardize error messages across components for user-facing consistency.
- Add stringent gateway request validation for content length, timeout clamping, and working directory allowlists.
- Enforce authentication for gateway execution via mandatory `GATEWAY_TOKEN` setup.
- Introduce cron and interval validation in task scheduler to ensure robust scheduling.
- Update `.gitignore` to include `.coverage` reports.
- Harden command execution by disallowing shell metacharacters in `backend.py`.
- Expand test coverage to validate error handling, security, timeout clamping, and `cwd` validation.

Merge pull request #4 from MegliolaLorenzo/feat/install-script

feat: add one-command installer (install.sh)

fix: address second review round + harden install.sh

- chmod 600 .env and tmpfiles (no world-readable secrets)
- set_env_var respects --dry-run
- pip install runs on every re-run (not only on venv creation)
- use $COMPOSE_CMD directly instead of make (docker compose compat)
- save gateway PID to .gateway.pid, clean stale pidfiles
- dry-run guard in "already exists" .env branch
- add --help flag
- bash 3.2 compatibility (COMPOSE_CMD as string, not array)

fix: dry-run prereq check and gateway deps fallback

- dry-run no longer exits when prerequisites are missing (warns instead)
- gateway setup falls back to hardcoded pip install if requirements.txt
  is not yet present (e.g. when cloning from main before merge)

Tested in Docker Ubuntu 24.04 with mock docker/make:
1. --dry-run on clean machine: OK
2. Full install with API key prompts: OK
3. Idempotent re-run: OK

fix: address PR review — dry-run crash, awk corruption, path consistency

- Guard `cd $INSTALL_DIR` for dry-run when directory doesn't exist yet
- Use awk ENVIRON instead of -v to prevent backslash interpretation in .env values
- Use absolute $INSTALL_DIR/.env paths everywhere (env_var_is_set, .env.example copy)
- Separate --no-prompt path to avoid printing interactive text in CI
- Add src/gateway/requirements.txt with pinned ranges (pydantic>=2)
- Makefile gateway target: always pip install --upgrade from requirements.txt
- Restore trailing newline in README.md

feat: add one-command installer (install.sh)

Single-command installer that handles the full setup:
- Prerequisites check (Docker, Compose, Git, Make, Python3)
- Repo clone or idempotent update
- Interactive .env setup with secret prompting
- Docker build and container startup
- Host gateway setup with launchd/systemd service option
- --dry-run and --no-prompt flags for preview and CI usage

Also includes supporting fixes:
- Makefile: gateway target uses venv for macOS compatibility
- server.py: load_dotenv() so gateway reads .env for GATEWAY_TOKEN
- .gitignore: add .venv/ and logs/
- README: add one-command install section, move manual setup to details

feat: add 14 new skills for host gateway bridges

Add skills for 1password, apple-reminders, bear-notes, blucli, camsnap,
imessage, obsidian, openhue, peekaboo, sonos, spotify, things,
troubleshooting, and whatsapp.

refactor: implement host gateway with client/server and host_execute tool

Move bridges from src/bridges/ to src/gateway/bridges/, add GatewayClient
(HTTP client for Docker-to-host communication), unified gateway server with
per-bridge command allowlists, and host_execute tool. Update config with
gateway section, add skill bridge filtering in middleware, remove obsolete
session-logs skill. Update all imports and tests.

test: expand test coverage across all major modules

Add comprehensive tests for gateway, bridge, middleware, telegram channel,
router, scheduler, config, rendering, and tools. Extends existing test
files with additional edge-case coverage and adds 13 new test modules.

refactor: extract ToolDetailsManager from duplicated tool-details logic

The expand/collapse tool-details code was duplicated character-for-character
in TelegramChannel (td: prefix) and ClaudeCodeHandler (cc: prefix). Extract
into a shared ToolDetailsManager class to eliminate duplication and make it
trivial for future bridges to reuse.

- New: src/channels/telegram/tool_details.py (ToolDetailsManager)
- New: tests/test_tool_details.py (13 tests)

docs: rebrand bridge system as secure host gateway architecture

Reframe the Host Bridge System from a narrow "forward messages to
Claude Code" feature to CianaParrot's core differentiator: Docker
sandboxing with controlled gateways to host OS apps.

- Update intro to emphasize sandboxed-but-connected architecture
- Add ASCII diagram showing sandboxed container vs host bridges
- Add security story (prompt injection containment)
- Add future bridges table (Apple Notes, Calendar, HomeKit, etc.)
- Update key features list with gateway-first language
docs: increase parrot icon size in README logo

docs: replace README logo with parrot + Syne text SVG

Uses font path outlines so the exact Syne Bold typeface renders on
GitHub without external font dependencies. Dark/light variants via
<picture> source media queries.
docs: add parrot SVG logo and cianaparrot.dev website badge to README

docs: update README to include DeepAgents framework reference

Add mention of DeepAgents framework with LangChain/LangGraph to the project description in the README.

feat: cc: commands in Telegram CC mode + improved timeout message

Add command system for Claude Code mode in Telegram (cc:help, cc:model,
cc:effort, cc:compact, cc:clear, cc:status, cc:cost). Extend UserSession
with model/effort preferences persisted across restarts. Improve timeout
message to indicate CC may still be working rather than showing an error.

chore: update .gitignore with runtime data entries

Add session_counters.json, data/sessions, data/checkpoints.db* to
gitignore. Move openclaw-main to .git/info/exclude (local-only).

feat: render Markdown tables as aligned monospace blocks in Telegram

Convert pipe-delimited Markdown tables to fenced code blocks before
HTML conversion, producing clean aligned columns with unicode separators.
Tables inside existing code blocks are left untouched.

feat: voice message transcription and photo support via Telegram

- Add multi-provider transcription service (Groq/OpenAI Whisper API)
- Accept voice, audio, and photo messages in Telegram channel
- Build multimodal HumanMessage for vision LLMs when photos are sent
- Proper MIME type handling for voice (.ogg) vs audio files (mp3, m4a)
- CC mode: voice transcribed and forwarded as text, photos rejected
- TranscriptionConfig with Pydantic validation and provider check
- 23 new tests covering transcription, config, and multimodal routing

feat: skill catalog with env-based auto-filtering + Dockerfile hardening

Add 13 new skills (weather, blogwatcher, gifgrep, notion, github, trello,
openai-image-gen, gemini-image-gen, elevenlabs-tts, google-places,
session-logs, video-frames, nano-pdf) with requires_env frontmatter field.

Skills requiring API keys are automatically skipped at load time when the
key is missing, and auto-enabled when the key is added to .env.

Middleware (src/middleware.py) monkey-patches DeepAgents' skill parser to:
- Pre-extract requires_env from raw YAML before DeepAgents strips it
- Skip skills with missing env vars
- Auto-quote broken YAML frontmatter values as fallback

Dockerfile optimized:
- Non-root user (app)
- HEALTHCHECK on checkpoint DB
- System deps: jq, ffmpeg, poppler-utils
- Dev deps separated into requirements-dev.txt
- .dockerignore added to shrink build context
Merge branch 'feature/skill-auto-creation' into main

feat: skill auto-creation, sandboxed execute, workspace isolation

- Mount skills inside workspace so agent can create its own skills at runtime
- Add WorkspaceShellBackend with allowlisted commands (curl, git, python, etc.)
- Move system data (checkpoints, sessions, counters) to data/ dir outside sandbox
- Sync session counters with existing checkpoints to prevent thread_id collisions
- Update agent identity with parrot personality and skill creation instructions
- Add curl to Docker image for agent web requests
docs: update changelog with v0.2.0 release notes

Merge pull request #2 from emanueleielo/feature/claude-code-telegram-bridge

Feature/claude code telegram bridge

docs: replace architecture PNG with SVG, clean up diagram references

- Replace static architecture.png with Excalidraw-exported SVG
- Remove message-flow and bridge-architecture image references from README
- Remove ASCII art diagram from bridge section
- Keep only architecture.svg and img.png as README images

docs: update README with bridge system, observability, and accurate project structure

- Add Host Bridge System section documenting the generic bridge architecture
  and Claude Code integration with full setup/usage guide
- Add Observability section for LangSmith tracing
- Update project structure tree to match actual package layout (telegram/,
  bridges/, events.py, store.py, agent_response.py, utils.py)
- Document all config sections: agent, web, logging, claude_code
- Add /cc commands to commands table
- Update model names to current (claude-sonnet-4-6)
- Add make test to Makefile section
- Add CC_BRIDGE_TOKEN to .env.example
- Include uncommitted rendering improvements: tool-specific icons,
  sub-agent collapsing, tool grouping, inline details button on response
  message, snake_case tool name support in events.py

feat: unify tool display between normal agent and Claude Code modes

Extract shared event types (TextEvent, ToolCallEvent, ThinkingEvent) and
rendering logic into src/events.py and src/channels/telegram/rendering.py,
used by both normal agent chat and CC mode.

Normal agent mode now shows tool calls with results, errors, thinking
blocks, and expandable "Tool details" inline buttons — identical to CC mode.

Also includes: session counter persistence via JsonStore, memory file path
fix (pass filename only to FilesystemBackend), conversation preview cleanup
(strip command XML tags), and simplified CC project/conversation list UI
(buttons only, no redundant text).

fix: apply code review findings across 10 files

- Remove dead code: unused import re, unused self._trigger, unused
  ConversationInfo.cwd field, unused JsonStore.update/clear methods
- Add logging to silent except blocks in CC handler
- Use set comprehension with max() for dedup trim instead of sorted()
- Validate session ID format before passing to --resume flag
- Add HTTP status check before parsing bridge response JSON
- Handle TimeoutError in CLI availability check
- Sanitize non-JSON CLI output (cap at 2000 chars, return as error)
- Use hmac.compare_digest for constant-time token comparison
- Strip version info from /health endpoint
- Convert web tools to async with httpx.AsyncClient
- Defer asyncio.Lock creation to init_cron_tools() (event loop safety)
- Track scheduler spawned tasks and drain on stop()
- Guard empty messages and handle list content in agent response

feat: improve CC bridge UI with conversations menu and better navigation

Replace the project-status reply button with a Conversations button that
opens the conversation list directly. Show the active project name in the
input placeholder instead. Add inline status buttons (Conversations,
Switch Project, Exit) when /cc is used while already in CC mode. Refactor
list views to support sending via message, edit, or chat_id through a
shared _send_list_view helper. Polish button labels with emoji and
shorten conversation previews.

fix: prevent reply keyboard from disappearing in CC mode

Restructure _process_message_locked so every code path re-establishes the
ReplyKeyboardMarkup:
- Fallback/multi-chunk paths now send response with reply keyboard and
  attach the tool-details inline button as a separate reply message
- Error/timeout paths always include reply_markup=keyboard
- New _send_response helper centralises the keyboard + inline split

Also improve tool details rendering:
- _render_cc_response returns per-event pre-formatted HTML items instead
  of a single markdown blob, enabling one message per tool/thinking block
- Expand/collapse now handles multiple detail messages
- Add strip_html_tags() fallback for BadRequest retries in channel.send()
- Fix code-block regex to require closing ``` on its own line

feat: add Claude Code bridge for Telegram

Introduce a bridge that lets users interact with Claude Code CLI
directly through Telegram. A host-side HTTP server proxies requests
to the Claude CLI, while the bot manages per-user sessions with
project selection and mode switching via ReplyKeyboard.

Key changes:
- Claude Code bridge (src/bridges/claude_code/) with HTTP server and
  async client that streams CLI output back to Telegram
- Telegram channel refactored into a package with extracted formatting,
  utilities, and a pluggable ModeHandler protocol
- Config migrated from raw dicts to Pydantic models with validation
- AgentResponse dataclass for structured responses with tool call info
- Cron tools use ContextVar and async locking to prevent race conditions
- Scheduler executes due tasks in parallel outside the lock
- Full test suite (pytest) covering config, routing, formatting,
  tools, bridge parsing, and handler rendering
Add Host Filesystem Access section to README
Translate Italian comments to English in config.yaml
Update LLM provider to claude-sonnet-4-6 and set temperature to 0

Protect runtime data and credentials from repository

Add allowed_users.example.json as template, gitignore the real file
to prevent leaking user IDs. Update gitignore for checkpoints.db
and scheduled_tasks.json.

Replace in-memory checkpointer with SQLite and enable filesystem sandboxing

Switch from MemorySaver to AsyncSqliteSaver so conversations persist
across container restarts. Enable virtual_mode=True on FilesystemBackend
to confine the agent to the workspace directory.
Update `.gitignore` to exclude `.claude` and `CLAUDE.md` files

Add Markdown-to-HTML conversion and improve Telegram message handling

- Convert Markdown (bold, italic, code, links, headers, blockquotes) to
  Telegram-compatible HTML before sending
- Fall back to plain text when HTML parsing fails
- Deduplicate incoming updates to prevent processing the same message twice
- Process messages in background tasks so the handler returns immediately
Update `architecture.png` diagram

Fix /new command to actually reset the session

The /new command was setting a flag in Telegram's chat_data context
that the router never read, so sessions were never actually reset.

Now the reset flows through the existing callback mechanism:
- Add `reset_session` field to IncomingMessage dataclass
- Telegram /new handler sends an IncomingMessage with reset_session=True
- Router checks the flag and calls reset_session() before processing

Narrow broad exception handlers to specific exception types

- main.py: catch OSError/RuntimeError for MCP client close
- router.py: catch JSONDecodeError/OSError for allowed_users loading
- scheduler.py: separate JSON/IO errors from unexpected exceptions,
  catch specific croniter parsing errors

Extract magic numbers to named constants

- TELEGRAM_MAX_MESSAGE_LEN in telegram.py
- MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH in web.py
- PROMPT_PREVIEW_LEN in cron.py (unify inconsistent 50/60 values)
Remove unused `_in_result` variable in DDGParser
Remove unused `sys` import from main.py
Merge pull request #1 from emanueleielo/claude/verify-skills-system-qYg5M

Fix misleading Skills system description in README

The previous claim "Drop a Python file in skills/ and it just works"
was inaccurate. A skill requires a subfolder with both SKILL.md
(metadata/instructions) and skill.py (tool functions with docstrings).
Update `img.png` file
Add UTC timestamp to formatted user messages in router logs

Initial release v0.1.0

DeepAgents-based AI assistant with Telegram, multi-provider LLM,
scheduled tasks, MCP support, skills system, and Docker deployment.

readme_html:
CianaParrot is a self-hosted AI personal assistant that runs on your own infrastructure — sandboxed inside Docker, but connected to your OS through secure bridges. Built on the DeepAgents framework with LangChain/LangGraph, it combines interactive chat via Telegram with autonomous scheduled tasks — all configured through a single YAML file.

Features

  • Multi-provider LLM — Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Groq, Ollama, OpenRouter, vLLM
  • Multi-tier model routing — Configure multiple LLM tiers (lite → expert) and let the agent switch models mid-conversation for cost optimization
  • Multi-channel — Pluggable channel architecture, Telegram out of the box
  • Host gateway — Secure bridge system connecting the Docker sandbox to host CLI tools (Spotify, Reminders, iMessage, Things, Bear Notes, Obsidian, 1Password, HomeKit, and more)
  • Scheduled tasks — Cron, interval, and one-shot tasks the agent can create via chat
  • Skills system — Drop a folder in skills/ and it auto-registers as agent tools
  • MCP support — Connect external MCP servers for unlimited extensibility
  • Persistent memory — Markdown-based identity and memory the agent updates itself
  • Observability — Optional LangSmith tracing
  • Docker-only deploy — One command to build, one to run

Architecture

Quick Start

One-command install

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emanueleielo/ciana-parrot/main/install.sh | bash

This handles everything: prerequisites check, repo clone, .env setup (prompts for API keys), Docker build, and host gateway startup.

Flags: bash install.sh --dry-run to preview without changes, --no-prompt for non-interactive/CI usage.

Manual setup

git clone https://github.com/emanueleielo/ciana-parrot.git
cd ciana-parrot
cp .env.example .env     # Edit with your API keys
make build && make up    # Build and start
make gateway             # Start host gateway (optional)

Open your bot on Telegram, send /start, and start chatting.

Documentation

Full documentation is available at docs.cianaparrot.dev, including:

  • Getting Started — Installation, setup, and first run
  • Architecture — Message flow, gateway system, persistence
  • Guides — Channels, bridges, skills, configuration, agent customization
  • Reference — API docs, config reference, Telegram commands
  • Contributing — Dev environment, testing, code style

License

MIT
